data.body:
SETTLED PROPOSAL.
PLAN-REVIEW threshold/posture changes (src/rebar/llm/plan_review/criteria_routing.json):
- Lower block_threshold 0.70->0.60: COH,E2,F1,G5,G6,T5e (had valid blocks in 0.6-0.7 band; band adj 15/20 block-worthy, 0 FP).
- Keep T4 @0.70 (fail-safe migrations = borderline zone).
- Promote to blocking(default_posture=blocking): G1G2@0.70, T1@0.70, T8@0.70 (100% block-worthy), E4@0.75 (0.75 excludes its lone borderline@0.716 -> 100% on sample).
- E1,T3: cannot reach 100% via threshold (borderline findings score >= valids; E1 borderline is top @0.835) -> keep advisory OR accept 75% @0.70. OPEN DECISION.
- A1 stays advisory (50%).
- Block-rate 21.1%->35.7% (36.6% if E1,T3 added). Flips 12->35 but recall-driven (addressed by finding-memory prime, Task2 approved).
- Task1 deterministic G5 child-signal: approved. Task2 Pass-1 finding-memory: approved.
- Filed idea neat-sooty-termite (c34d): rework impact aggregation (mean-of-5 dilutes high-severity below bar).
CODE-REVIEW (revised after user correction: goal=long-term maintainability not clean diff): maintainability adj of 31 findings = 7 HIGH-VALUE / 24 NIT / 0 FP. Priority CANNOT separate (landmines #10/#19 at 0.415 floor; doc-ahead nits up to 0.668) => NO threshold blocks landmines without nits. So: (a) do NOT introduce priority-threshold LLM blocking now; (b) block a semantic class 'new behavior/contract shipped without test' (DET-style, needs feasibility spike) = highest-value landmine buried at 0.415; (c) cut nit friction: docs+llm-prompts = nit-factories (0/9 high-value), down-rank/suppress esp 'doc-ahead-of-landed-sibling' pattern; (d) keep DET secret+high-crit blocking; (e) revisit priority-blocking after impact-aggregation rework. OPEN: landmine-gate approach (DET detector vs finding-class tag).
